title: [HGLDD] Don't access instance ports as hierarchical path
body:
HGLDD emission currently emits instance output ports as `bar.a` hierarchical paths, instead of pointing to the wire that is attached to `bar`'s port `a`. Consider this example:
```mlir
hw.module @Foo(out x : i36, out y : i36) {
  %a, %b = hw.instance "bar" @Bar() -> (a: i36, b: i36)
  hw.output %a, %b : i36, i36
}
hw.module.extern @Bar(out a : i36, out b : i36) attributes {verilogName = "Bar"}
```
Running this through `firtool -g --emit-hgldd reduced15.mlir` produces the following problematic HGLDD JSON snippet:
```
"value": {"field":"a","var_ref":{"sig_name":"bar"}},
"value": {"field":"b","var_ref":{"sig_name":"bar"}},
```
In this case, the `{"sig_name":"bar"}` is trying to refer to the instance of `Bar`, which is invalid. Instead, EmitHGLDD should do the following:
- Also look at the `hw.output` op to see if an instance output is directly used as a module's output, and use that output's name in the HGLDD. We already do this for local wires.
- Never emit the `{"field":"a","var_ref":{"sig_name":"bar"}}` style of hierarchical path access into instances. Instead, just drop the instance port if it cannot be materialized.
